entry.title: SAP Business Unleashed: A Look into the Future
entry.content:
Digitalization is changing our working environment faster than ever before. With SAP Business Unleashed, SAP has provided an exciting outlook on the future of its business software – with many new opportunities for companies to optimize their processes and make them more efficient.

The Way Forward: Data, AI and Integration

SAP pursues a clear strategy: use data intelligently, use artificial intelligence (AI sensibly, and connect different systems seamlessly. A central component of this is the SAP Business Data Cloud , which helps companies link internal and external data sources and thus make better decisions.

Particularly exciting is Joule , an AI-powered copilot that is designed to automate business processes and support teams in their daily work. Joule promises to not only take on repetitive tasks, but also provide valuable insights that make decisions easier.

What makes the new SAP strategy strong?

1. Real-time data and analytics Thanks to SAP Business Data Cloud, companies can not only collect data, but also analyze it in real time. This means faster reactions to market changes and more informed decisions.

2. Artificial intelligence as a game changer With Joule, SAP is launching an AI solution that can do much more than simple automation. It understands processes, analyzes data and supports teams in their daily work - a real competitive advantage.

3. Openness and integration SAP no longer only relies on its own solutions, but is increasingly integrating with other platforms - for example through its partnership with Databricks . This gives companies more flexibility to further develop their existing systems instead of replacing them completely.

4. Real-world examples Henkel, Bosch and other companies have shown how they use SAP technologies to optimize their data strategy and thus work more efficiently. These examples make it clear that SAP is not just talking about the future, but is already putting it into practice.

 

Still open questions: What remains unclear?

Despite the promising developments, there are some aspects that should be examined in more detail:

1. How is the implementation carried out?

  • What concrete steps do companies need to take to integrate the SAP Business Data Cloud into their existing IT landscape?
  • What challenges might arise during implementation and how can they be solved?

2. Data protection and security

  • What measures has SAP taken to comply with data protection guidelines and minimize security risks?
  • How is sensitive company data protected from unauthorized access?

3. Costs and ROI

  • What investments are required to benefit from the new SAP technologies?
  • Are there already concrete figures or experience reports that illustrate the return on investment (ROI)?

Conclusion: Set the course for tomorrow now

With SAP Business Unleashed , it is clear that SAP wants to help companies work even more intelligently and in a more connected way. AI, data integration and flexible platforms are no longer a thing of the future - they are now available. Companies that rely on them early on will secure clear competitive advantages.

entry.title: Re: Server Migration of SAP BI 4.3 SP4 Windows server 201...
entry.content:
Hi sgore,

I faced a similar scenario in the past. As a mandatory step you need to do a fresh SAP BO 4.3 installation on W22 servers (be sure that you are installing "exactly" the same release-patch you installed on the W19 servers).

At that point you have to options to move the full repository content from W19 to W22 SAP BO install

1) To perform a full system promotion as described below using Command Line Interface (CLI)

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_BUSINESSOBJECTS_BUSINESS_INTELLIGENCE_PLATFORM/2e167338c1b24da9b2a94e68efd79c42/033c81d383af4947ac8d56e68cde1522.html?locale=en-US&state=PRODUCTION&version=4.3.4

This is a well know approach and you will find plenty of references about it including some interesting guides such this one

https://help.sap.com/doc/pattern-book-on-life-cycle-managementpromotion-management/4.2/en-US/8%20Pattern%20Book%20on%20Life%20Cycle%20Management-%20Promotion%20Management.pdf

2) Create a cluster. This is a very clever approach. Consists on create a cluster of CMS. When doing it all the content is "automatically" synchronized across both set of servers. Once systems are aligned, remove W19 servers from the cluster and shutdown them.

This second approach is poor documented and it´s a bit messy but provides an straight forward path if you are able to complete it successfully (it's my favourite one)

General recommendations (for whatever approach you use): Take backups of servers and test all process steps in advance on a test environment.

Regards,

Alfons

entry.title: SAP Business Data Cloud: Unifying Enterprise Data for Next-Generation Insights
entry.content:
Introduction

SAP’s Business Data Cloud (BDC) is a new cloud-based offering designed to unify data from various SAP applications and beyond, giving business users a single, consistent view of information. With BDC, enterprises can integrate and analyze data from multiple sources more efficiently, eliminating the hassle of juggling disconnected tools. The result: faster, more reliable insights to make better business decisions.

Why Business Data Cloud?

  1. Simplicity: Instead of manually stitching together data from different systems, BDC automates data integration using a concept called “Data Products.”
  2. Out-of-the-Box Insights: BDC provides pre-built “Insight Apps,” which let you see and explore your data right away.
  3. Openness: BDC can bring in data from both SAP and non-SAP sources. You get a more comprehensive picture of your organization’s data without dealing with multiple, siloed solutions.

Key Concepts

1. Data Products

A “Data Product” is a curated dataset drawn from SAP or other systems, ready for immediate use. These datasets contain everything you need for analytics or AI tasks—no extra wrangling required. They’re designed with standardized structures so they can be easily reused and shared.

2. BDC Foundation Services

These behind-the-scenes services handle the heavy lifting:

  • Data Extraction: Pulling information from source systems like SAP S/4HANA.
  • Transformation: Cleaning and structuring the data so it’s analytics-ready.
  • Publishing: Making Data Products available for consumption via modern protocols like Delta Sharing.

Foundation Services are internal to SAP, so you won’t see them in the user interface. Instead, they make sure your Data Products are always up-to-date and accessible.

3. SAP BDC (The Customer-Facing Part)

SAP BDC is the “front door” that customers actually use. It includes:

  • BDC Cockpit: A single dashboard for administrators to configure data pipelines, onboard data sources, and manage user access.
  • Analytics & Visualization: Built on SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud, so you can build dashboards, run analytics, and even do planning—without juggling separate tools.
  • Insight Apps: Pre-built templates that deliver out-of-the-box reports and analytics. For instance, you might get a ready-made “Procurement Insight App” for spend analysis.

4. Integration with Existing SAP Solutions

BDC leverages existing technologies like SAP HANA Cloud and Spark, plus new features such as HANA Data Lake Files (HDLF), to manage and store data. It also integrates with Databricks (optional) for advanced data engineering and machine learning scenarios.

What Problems Does BDC Solve?

  • Data Silos: BDC pulls data from across your organization into one place, cutting down on duplication and conflicting data definitions.
  • Complex Setups: With a single cockpit, you can set up data connections and maintain them centrally, instead of dealing with multiple configuration screens in different tools.
  • Slow Insights: By standardizing how data is published, you can quickly spin up analytics or AI projects—spending more time on innovation and less time on setup.

Looking Ahead

BDC represents SAP’s ongoing evolution to meet modern data needs. In the future, expect even deeper integration with AI and machine learning platforms, broader support for non-SAP data sources, and expanded partner solutions.

Getting Started

  1. Identify Data Sources: Decide which SAP or third-party systems contain the information you need.
  2. Check Available Insight Apps: Explore pre-built analytics templates that might already deliver the insights you want.
  3. Use BDC Cockpit: Manage data onboarding, security, and user access—all in one place.

Conclusion

SAP Business Data Cloud aims to simplify data integration and empower both technical and non-technical users to tap into real-time, trusted data. With pre-built Data Products, a robust foundation for data sharing, and user-friendly analytics tools, BDC helps organizations quickly uncover insights and make smarter decisions.

For more detailed technical information, refer to SAP’s internal architecture documents on BDC.

entry.title: Re: multiple brands for one material in SAP MM
entry.content:
The best approach depends on business requirements, but using Split Valuation
is generally the preferred solution in SAP MM. This allows a single material code to
represent multiple brands while distinguishing them through valuation types. Each brand can have its own
valuation type, enabling separate stock tracking, pricing, and valuation without creating duplicate material codes.
This approach reduces master data maintenance and simplifies procurement and inventory management. However, if different brands require distinct attributes,
BOMs, or procurement processes, separate material codes might be justified. Overall, Split Valuation by Brand
ensures better efficiency and streamlined data management.

entry.title: Re: How to Configure "standard text key" for task list &...
entry.content:
To configure the Standard Text Key for an Equipment Task List (IA01) and Maintenance Order (IW31), follow these steps:
1. Define Standard Text Key: Go to SPRO → Plant Maintenance and Customer Service → Maintenance Plans, Work Centers, Task Lists → Task Lists → Define Standard Text Key and create a new key.
2. Assign Standard Text Key to Task List: In IA01, enter the Standard Text Key in the operation details under the External Processing tab.
3. Use in Maintenance Order: In IW31, when selecting a task list, the Standard Text Key will be copied into the order operation.
4. Maintain Standard Texts: Use transaction SO10 to create predefined text entries that can be linked to the Standard Text Key.
5. Test and Validate: Create a task list and a maintenance order to ensure the text key functions correctly.

entry.title: Futuristic Proposals: Elevating SAP Sales Cloud V2 with SAP Business Data Cloud
entry.content:
As enterprises continue to demand real‑time insights and seamless data integration, SAP Sales Cloud V2 is evolving to meet these challenges. Looking forward, integrating the powerful capabilities of SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) can drive transformative change—making Sales Cloud V2 not only smarter but also more agile and secure.

1. Real‑Time Data Products for Instant Insights

BDC’s data products are pre‑curated, governed datasets that capture critical business information in real time. By integrating these with SAP Sales Cloud V2, sales teams can access:

  • Live Customer Data: Instantly view the most current customer interactions and behaviors.
  • Dynamic Pipeline Visibility: Leverage continuously updated sales opportunity data to drive faster decision‑making.

Such real‑time insights help sales teams anticipate customer needs and react swiftly to market changes, ensuring that decisions are always data‑driven.

2. Enhanced AI and Predictive Analytics

BDC’s integration with cutting‑edge AI platforms, including Databricks, enables the creation of advanced insight apps that run predictive models on unified data sets. For example:

  • Predictive Sales Forecasting: AI‑driven models can analyze historical and current sales data to forecast trends and identify high‑value opportunities.
  • Next‑Best Action Recommendations: Sales representatives receive real‑time suggestions based on customer behavior and market dynamics.

These capabilities are already being showcased in recent SAP announcements, where AI insights are turbocharging business decision‑making citeturn0search15.

3. Unified Customer Data Ecosystem

Integrating BDC with Sales Cloud V2 provides a unified, single‑version-of‑the‑truth for customer data:

  • Consistent Data Governance: With BDC’s robust versioning and data governance, sales data remains accurate and secure across multiple systems.
  • Seamless Integration Across SAP Solutions: Whether it’s data from SAP S/4HANA, SAP Ariba, or third‑party sources, BDC harmonizes disparate data into one accessible ecosystem citeturn0search8.

This unified approach reduces data silos and ensures that sales strategies are built on reliable, high‑quality data.

4. Customizable and Adaptive Insight Apps

BDC’s framework supports the development of both standard and custom insight apps:

  • Tailored Sales Dashboards: Pre‑built templates and customizable apps enable sales managers to monitor KPIs, pipeline health, and performance metrics.
  • Low-Code/No-Code Enhancements: Leveraging platforms like SAP Build, organizations can quickly adapt the solution to meet unique business requirements without heavy development overhead citeturn0search17.

Such flexibility ensures that as market needs evolve, your sales cloud remains agile and responsive.

5. Automated Data Governance and Security

As sales data becomes increasingly valuable, securing it is paramount. BDC’s advanced data governance features can:

  • Automate Compliance: Enforce data protection standards and automate data lifecycle management.
  • Ensure Data Integrity: Use semantic versioning and controlled updates to protect the integrity of sales data.

This foundation of trust and security is critical for organizations operating in regulated industries.

6. Future‑Proof Architecture

Integrating BDC with SAP Sales Cloud V2 offers a forward‑looking, microservices‑based architecture:

  • Scalability and Flexibility: The cloud‑native design allows the system to scale effortlessly, accommodating growing data volumes and evolving business needs.
  • Continuous Innovation: With BDC’s ability to rapidly deploy new AI models and insight apps, organizations can continually innovate without disruption.

This future‑proof architecture not only supports current requirements but also positions your sales cloud to adapt to emerging trends and technologies.

Conclusion

By harnessing the power of SAP Business Data Cloud, SAP Sales Cloud V2 can be transformed into a truly best‑in‑class solution. Real‑time data products, AI‑driven insights, a unified customer data ecosystem, customizable apps, automated governance, and a future‑proof architecture collectively enable sales teams to work smarter, faster, and more securely. As organizations look to gain a competitive edge in an ever‑evolving market, integrating BDC with Sales Cloud V2 is a strategic move toward a data‑driven, agile future.

Embrace these futuristic proposals and unlock the full potential of your sales organization.

entry.title: Re: S4HANA Cloud Create Outgoing Invoices (FB70) - how to...
entry.content:
In S/4HANA Cloud, the Item Long Text (Notes) entered in FB70 (Create Outgoing Invoices) is typically stored in the long text table (STXL)
and linked via object key references. However, standard CDS views may not always expose this directly.
To retrieve the Item Long Text, try using:
1. CDS View I_JournalEntryItemText – This might provide text details for journal entries.
2. Read Text using Read_Text Function Module (for on-premise, limited in Cloud).
3. Check Extensibility – Use Custom Fields and Logic (APP ID: F1481) to extend standard views.
4. Explore API: Journal Entry - Read (A2X) – This may provide text-related details.

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud for group reporting - Release 2502 is generally available
entry.content:
We're glad to announce the release of SAP S/4HANA for group reporting 2502 on February 15, 2025, with new and improved features to help you run your corporate close process. This new release introduces several key innovations, significantly enhancing the data collection process through new features for the Group Reporting Data Collection app. The 2502 release also helps you simplify data quality controls with the integration of multiple line and balance validations. Additionally, the analytics review booklet has been improved to include superior commenting capabilities and AI-assisted insights. A new app offering an analytical group view on unreleased accounting data has also been introduced, which streamlines your reporting processes. Furthermore, connectivity to the SAP Green Ledger is now available, facilitating better management of greenhouse gas emissions at consolidated level.

Download our feature deck.

Key innovations in release Group Reporting 2502 include:

  • Launch your controls from within the Group Reporting Data Collection package
  • Easy navigation between Group Reporting Data Collection forms
  • Defining several fiscal years, periods, and versions for one Group Reporting Data Collection form
  • Advanced monitoring and import alerts in Group Reporting Data Collection's mapping app
  • Improved data mapping logs in Group Reporting Data Collection
  • Multiple line validation tasks on non-consolidated data to enhance your efficiency in data quality
  • Multiple balance validation tasks on non-consolidated data to enhance your efficiency in data quality
  • Extending usability in creating manual journals using replace feature
  • Review booklet, improved analytical cell comments and notifications
  • Review booklet, AI-assisted financial business insights for group statements (beta)
  • New app with an analytical group view on accounting data
  • Streamlining sustainability reporting by integrating SAP Green Ledger with Group Reporting
  • New master data capabilities to delete financial statement items 
  • Enhanced user experience to display master data change of financial statement items
  • Tight accounting integration, option to release data from prior periods
  • New master data features including read APIs and consolidation hierarchy management

--
Group Reporting Data Collection: Launch your data collection controls from within the package

With release 2502, we integrate Group Reporting Data Collection (GRDC) package with the data monitor task execution. Now, you can directly run the controls and perform a series of data monitor tasks directly from within the package. This key innovation is part of our roadmap acceleration program, which aims at developing the best Financial Consolidation features in Group Reporting. 

With this innovation, you benefit from an increased efficiency and embedded data quality controls in your data collection process.

In the "Enter Package Data" app of Group Reporting Data Collection:

  • The forms navigation tree is enhanced with new actions
  • You can run group reporting tasks as an action (release of data, annual net-income calculation, and line validations)
  • Display the status of a task directly in the app
  • Access the last log of a task execution from within the app

How does it work? In the "Manage Package" app, you can define data entry steps based on tasks defined in the data monitor. Tasks from the data monitor are integrated with the Manage Packages app, and are then displayed in the Enter Package Data app. When you enter a version, start fiscal year, and period, one step is automatically created and the tasks for this step are populated with the tasks from the data monitor in your SAP S/4HANA system. You can define additional steps and tasks. For each task, you can assign folders, and define read and write document type values centrally.

For more information: Manage Packages.

--
Group Reporting Data Collection: Easy navigation between forms

Also part of our roadmap acceleration program, in 2502, you benefit from an enhanced navigation with a "navigate-to" feature between forms within a package, to provide more guidance when entering data in forms.

You can define links between forms at design time, in the "Manage Forms" app. Single or multiple target forms are supported. When opening the form in the "Enter Package Data" app, the cells that offer navigation are indicated by a triangle. Just open the target form when navigating to it from the source form.

For more information: Create a Shortcut.

--
Defining several fiscal years, periods, and versions for one Group Reporting Data Collection form

Also part of our roadmap acceleration program, with 2502 you can now define several fiscal years, periods, and versions for one form in Group Reporting Data Collection. Use this feature if you need to enter data of different fiscal years and periods in one single form for your management reporting, or display comparative data in a form (the previous month or a comparison version, for instance), which can include formulas that calculate the difference between the current period and the comparative period, year, or version.

This new feature addresses multiple use cases, such as:

  • When working on the current actuals period, you want to see the prior year data of the same period in the form as a display column
  • When working on the current actuals period, you want to see the prior period of the same year in the form as a display column
  • When working on a restatement of the prior year in an extension version, you want to enter data for all periods of the year at the same time, both in periodic or year-to-date mode
  • When working on the financial plan of the next year(s) in a plan version, you want to enter data for fiscal years or for the budget all 12 months of the year at the same time (screenshot below, where months are in column)
  • When working on the current actuals period, you want to see the corresponding budget version in the form as a display column.

For more information: Multiple Periods and Multiple Fiscal Years.

--
Advanced monitoring and import alerts in Group Reporting Data Collection's mapping app

With the advanced monitoring and import alerts in Group Reporting Data Collection, you can now monitor all last job runs in a single view and benefit from new capabilities, including a run status, visual display of errors, warnings, information and successes in micro-charts, and direct access to logs and files. Within the same app, you can also define users to be notified by email depending on the job run status. Key features include:

  • Complete overview of the upload process at one glance
  • Detailed information about the last job runs
  • Enhanced usability of the job list
  • One-click access to logs and files
  • Notifications and alerts

For more information: Advanced Monitoring and Import Alerts.

--
Improved data mapping logs in Group Reporting Data Collection

With 2502, you can leverage improved job processing, traceability log, and mapping log features in the "Data Mapping" app of Group Reporting Data Collection. These enhancements simplify, streamline, and secure the mapping and import process for operations in production environments. They also make processes and logs easier to understand and analyze.

Key improvements include:

  • Enhanced job processing
    – Automatically derive the local currency key from consolidation unit master data during import
    – Define import options in the mapping definition (instead of in the job)
    – Streamline job run header information and move additional information into a separate tab
    – Support .txt extension files as a source

  • Enhanced traceability log
    – Remove the limit of 30 million characters
    – New sheet with job run information such as that displayed in the job run Overview tab
    – New mapping file sheets to the traceability log file
    – Improved sorting and filtering capabilities (for example, removal of merged cells in header rows and change data types, default freeze panes)
    – Display mapping messages before mapping rules and the import messages before output fields
    – Ease the filtering of messages to only display errors and warning

  • Mapping log (screenshot below)
    – Display error and warning messages at the top
    – Display the name of the field and the value that is the origin of the error or warning
    – Remove unnecessary information messages from the mapping log
    – Improve the quality of error messages to provide more helpful guidance to users
    – Display the name of the field and the value that is the origin of the error or warning
    – Remove unnecessary information messages from the mapping log
    – Improve the quality of error messages to provide more helpful guidance to users

For more information: Run Information.

--
Multiple line validation tasks on non-consolidated data to enhance your efficiency in managing data quality control

Line-item validation is a preventive control to help you ensure data quality of non-consolidated data, either entered with Group Reporting Data Collection, or directly integrated from general accounting. It automatically checks if a combination of field values is allowed. A typical line-item validation message would be “Consolidation unit S001 doesn’t allow Profit center PC002”.

In release 2502, we release multiple line-validation tasks on group reporting and unreleased accounting data to improve your efficiency in controlling data quality in different scenarios such as:

  • Staggered data collection process, with submission and validation of different data sets at different dates (such as profit and loss first, balance sheet later)
  • Loading of remote system data into group reporting, running validations as separate tasks, and deciding to correct errors either at the source or in group reporting
  • Smooth and fast group closing process by running quality checks on unreleased accounting data early, right after the accounting posting becomes available, and applying the group reporting quality definitions

--
Multiple balance validation tasks on non-consolidated data to enhance your efficiency in managing data quality control

Balance validation helps you ensure data quality during the period-end close of your group. It validates data completeness, correctness, and compliance of balance sheet and P&L. You can define balance validation rules to check two selected amounts against each other, whether financial amounts or quantities. A typical example would be “Assets equal Liabilities and Equity”.

In release 2502, you benefit from enhanced balance validation task abilities, improving your efficiency in controlling data quality in different scenarios, such as:

  • Staggered data collection process, with submission and validation of different data sets at different dates, such as profit and loss first and balance sheet later, leveraging multiple balance validation tasks
  • Smooth and fast group closing process by running quality checks on unreleased accounting data early, right after the accounting posting becomes available, and applying the group reporting quality definitions to unreleased accounting data
  • Higher efficiency in rule creation when more than three group-by fields are needed
  • Higher usability in rule group assignment to consolidation units and groups leveraging selection object features, such as select by consolidation unit attribute or hierarchy

You can now validate balance selections in multiple tasks and run group reporting rule definitions on unreleased accounting data, group reporting data on the consolidation unit level, and on the consolidation group level.

You can use a new configuration interface to define balance validation tasks, allowing you to create multiple tasks as needed to separate validation rules by different user ownership or steps in the closing process, and assign execution types to tasks to determine the data source (unreleased accounting data, consolidation unit level, or consolidation group level).

A new balance validation infrastructure for financial data is available for group reporting, including new or adapted apps.

The rule definition app is replaced with a new SAP Fiori app for validation rule and group definitions, reusing the accounting balance validation rule definition app in Group Reporting.  Existing group reporting validation rules and methods are migrated to this new app.

Also, you can now use a new SAP Fiori app to assign a validation rule group to a validation task based on versions, periods, and consolidation units or groups. Existing method assignments are migrated to this new app.

Additionally, other new features for balance validation include the use of up to six group-by fields in rules definition, assignment of rule groups to consolidation units and groups, and support for multiple languages for rule description fields.

For more information, check out our new balance validation blog and see how to:

  • Run balance validation and view results
  • Manage balance validation rules and rule groups
  • Assign rule group for balance validation
  • Import/Export validation settings

--
Extending usability in creating manual journals using replace feature

In 2502, you benefit from an enhanced usability of the Post Group Journal Entries app, with a new Replace button to reverse and copy a manual journal entry in draft mode. It sometimes happen that you make a wrong manual journal entry, or need to update it. The Replace feature enables you to do so, while keeping the original entry to ensure audit continuity and completeness.

The Replace action is available on the overview screen of the Post Group Journal Entries app. It combines the Reverse and Copy actions. The reversal document is posted in the background, and a draft of the copied document is prepared, allowing the user to make modifications.

If the reversal is part of a workflow, the system creates a workflow item in the background. The posting then waits for approval (suggestion: “the posting must be approved”). If the new document isn’t part of a workflow, it might be posted before the reversal. If you decide to replace a document that is part of a document bundle, the system replaces the entire bundle.

  1. The original document references the reversal document in the Reversed With field.
  2. The reversal document references the original document in the Reversed Document field.
  3. The corrected document does not have any reference.

For more information: Post Group Journal Entries

--
Review booklet, improved analytical cell comments and notifications

With 2502, you benefit from improved commenting capabilities in the group financial statement review booklet.

Key new features include:

  • Analytical cell-based comments
    – Ability to discuss numbers in certain cell
    – Categorize, tag, filter and edit
    – History of comments and contributions
    – Life cycle status for each comment
    – Comments of all loaded cells
  • Show all loaded comments on a page (screenshot above)
    – Rename “Cell Comment Header”
    – Classify and filter comments

  • Comments of selected cells
    – Add and view comment via context menu
    – Click on comment to see detailed history

In addition, you can use the new "@Mention" feature in your comments (screenshot below). Your mentioned colleagues are notified about your comment in the in-app notification and via email.

For more information: watch our video. 

--
Review booklet, AI-assisted financial business insights for group statements (beta)

Part of SAP's vision for Business AI, we deliver a new AI-assisted feature for financial business insights for group financial statement review booklet. This new beta feature embeds generative AI in the Review Booklet to help you distill insights on group performance, identify outliers in large quantities of data, and summarize comments in a fraction of the time.

Key capabilities include:

  • Analyze and summarize tabular data on a review booklet page
  • Identify and evaluate relevant business situations
  • Highlight findings within large datasets
  • Create a management summary of group-level financial data
  • Include comments and calculate KPIs

You can experience generative AI in the Review Booklet and influence our Group Reporting business AI roadmap by applying to our beta program.

--
New app with an analytical group view on accounting data

From the perspective of the integration of consolidation with general accounting, in Group Reporting 2502, we introduce a new application with an analytical group view on accounting data. With this new app you can analyze data in a group view directly on accounting data for the following scenarios:

  • Anytime reporting on accounting data in a group view reflecting the latest posting documents from accounting. You can combine the reporting with prediction and transactional consolidation ledgers from accounting to offer forward-looking consolidated management views based on the latest accounting data.
  • Analysis reporting for companies submitting their data to group reporting. For example, checking for unreleased data and analyzing the impact on reports like P&L of their next data release to group reporting.

These early insights can be valuable for accessing the latest accounting data. This way, you can make informed decisions based on current accounting information in the group language.

Key features:

  • Group view on latest accounting material
  • Reflects all accounting journals
  • Translated @spot rate of the transaction
  • Presented in your group definitions
  • Filter parameters: consolidation group, consolidation unit, GR posting periods, among others
  • Reporting row structures driven by FS item hierarchies
  • Reporting rule definitions and reporting item hierarchies (for example Cash Flow Statement)
  • Predefined column structures for period, quarter, year comparison

For more information: Group View on Accounting Review Booklet

--
Streamlining sustainability reporting by integrating SAP Green Ledger with SAP Group Reporting

With release 2502, you can further integrate your greenhouse gas emission reporting process on group level. You can leverage new features to collect greenhouse gas information, eliminate intercompany transactions both in quantity (for example tons of carbon emissions) and amount, and produce a consolidated sustainability reporting.

In addition to the five specific fields for greenhouse gas (GHG) emission reporting already supported within group reporting – GHG Scope, GHG Category, GHG Scope 2 Calculation Method, GHG Scope 2 Contractual Instrument and Sustainability Mode of Transport – you can now use four new fields:

  • GHG Data Quality Characteristic
  • GHG Energy Classification
  • GHG Energy Mix
  • GHG Energy Sourcing Type

Furthermore, with release 2502, we help you streamline your sustainability reporting by integrating SAP Green Ledger with SAP Group Reporting. This integration is enabled by using the data mapping apps within SAP Group Reporting Data Collection.

Given the increasing importance of sustainability reporting, driven by investor and regulatory scrutiny, you may face challenges in navigating complex regulations like IFRS S2 and require robust data management solutions.

SAP Green Ledger captures granular environmental data, while SAP Group Reporting is our platform for consolidated financial and environmental reporting. Today, we integrate these systems to provide comprehensive sustainability insights.

The data mapping apps within SAP Group Reporting Data Collection allow you to:

  • Connect SAP Green Ledger and SAP Group Reporting by enabling the ETL (extraction, transformation, and load) of environmental data from SAP Green Ledger into the SAP Group Reporting consolidation table (ACDOCU)
  • Seamlessly configure an OData service within SAP Green Ledger to expose the necessary data
  • Create data mapping definitions using mapping files to define rules for transforming data from SAP Green Ledger for the consolidation table (see screenshot below)
  • Automate the import process and generate the required reports

By leveraging data mapping apps to integrate SAP Green Ledger with SAP Group Reporting, you can:

  • Streamline sustainability reporting processes
  • Meet regulatory requirements like IFRS S2
  • Gain valuable insights into your environmental impact
  • Make more informed business decisions

This integration empowers your organization to move beyond basic compliance and use sustainability data to drive strategic initiatives, creating a more sustainable future.

For more information:

  • Streamlining Sustainability Reporting: Integrating SAP Green Ledger with SAP Group Reporting
  • Group Reporting Fields for Greenhouse Gas Emissions
  • Group-level Green Ledger - managing greenhouse gas emissions for group reporting

--
New master data capabilities to delete financial statement items 

In release 2502, you benefit from improved usability in managing financial statement items. A new feature in the "Define Financial Statements" app allows you to delete financial statement items. This feature is secured by a "Check Where Used" capability that prevents the deletion of financial statement items that are still in use.

You can delete financial statement items on both the overview and details screens of the "Define Financial Statement Items" app. This applies to customer-defined financial statement items as well as those delivered with SAP content.

For data and master data security reasons, you may only delete financial statement items that are no longer in use and that you are authorized to delete. If either of these prerequisites is violated, an error message explaining the reason why the financial statement item could not be deleted will be displayed.

If you cannot delete a financial statement item because it is still in use, you can utilize the "Check Where Used" functionality within the "Define Financial Statement Items" app. This will provide information about the usage of the financial statement item. After removing all usages of a financial statement item, you can then proceed with its deletion.

For more information: Delete FS Items in the 'Define FS Items' App.

--
Enhanced user experience to display master data change of financial statement items

Tracking master data changes is crucial for several reasons. Firstly, it enhances operational efficiency by maintaining a single source of truth, reducing the risk of errors caused by data inconsistencies. Tracking changes aids in compliance and risk management, helping organizations meet regulatory requirements and mitigate risks related to data breaches or inaccuracies. Moreover, it is particularly important during audits, as auditors need to verify the accuracy and consistency of data over time. Overall, a clear, precise, and usable app to display master data changes enhances your experience by ensuring consistent and accurate information.

Part of 2502 release, we introduce the ability to display master data change log information in the “Define Financial Statement Items” app, offering unprecedented access to detailed change logs of financial statement items.

One of the key aspects of the new "Show Change Log" feature is its user-friendly interface. Unlike the technical backend transaction, which required knowledge of database table names, the new integrated access is designed with the end-user in mind. This means that users can now easily access and understand changes made to financial statement items without needing technical expertise.

The change log information is meticulously organized for your enhanced readability. Changes are grouped by timestamp, user, and type of change, making it straightforward to track modifications. The log includes all master data attributes, such as time- and version-independent attributes, time- and version-dependent attributes, and language-dependent attributes. This comprehensive coverage ensures that users have a complete view of all changes made to financial statement items.

Filtering options further enhance the usability of this feature. You can filter the displayed change log information by various dimensions, including date, username, language, and attribute. This allows for targeted analysis and quick identification of specific changes. Additionally, you can export the change log information.

For public cloud customers, this feature marks the first time you have access to the master data change log of financial statement items. This is a significant step forward in providing comprehensive and accessible financial management tools to a broader audience.

The "Define Financial Statement Items" app's new feature for displaying master data change log information is a powerful tool that enhances transparency, accountability, and usability in financial statement management. By providing a user-friendly interface, comprehensive attribute coverage, and robust filtering options, this feature empowers you to efficiently track and understand changes in master data.

More information: Display Master Data Change Log for FS Item.

--
Tight accounting integration, option to release data from prior periods

In the 2502 release, you now have the option to release data from prior periods in the “Release of Universal Documents for Consolidation” app. This new feature increases efficiency by integrating late accounting data from previous periods, such as postings made after the previous period's closure in group reporting (“Delta”). Additionally, it aids in activating group reporting on productive accounting environments with initial year-to-date data integration (“Complete Data”).

Use the “Delta” mode to integrate late accounting data. When the group close has commenced, late postings in general accounting may occur. This data from prior periods, which may already be closed, needs processing. You can achieve this seamlessly using the new option to release data from prior periods. This feature eliminates the need to reopen and reprocess the “Release Universal Journal” task in the current period mode, as well as all subsequent consolidation tasks in respective periods. The prior periods mode option allows you to process accounting data from closed periods and transfer the results into the current execution period.

Use the “Complete Data” mode when you want to activate group reporting on a productive accounting environment, transferring opening balances. This mode is particularly relevant for creating a starting point for a consolidation unit in group reporting.

You can use the new option to release data from prior periods on-demand via your “Data Monitor,” or schedule a job using the “Schedule Jobs for Consolidation Tasks” app.

For more information: Release Universal Journal: Support of Prior Periods Mode.

--
New master data features including read APIs and consolidation hierarchy management

With release 2502, you also benefit from additional new features that improve your master data management.

  • New API for reading group reporting transactional data with a reporting item – Increases flexibility in extracting data from group reporting with a reporting item that gives access to advanced reports such as the cash flow statement in an API call and improves performance compared to the old API. More information: Consolidation Global Parameter.

  • Display unassigned master data values feature in the "Manage Consolidation Hierarchies" app – You can now display master data values which are not (yet) assigned to a consolidation hierarchy in the “Manage Consolidation Hierarchies” app. This feature is available for all consolidation hierarchy types except “Consolidation Posting Level” hierarchies. 
    More information: Manage Consolidation Hierarchies.

  • Aggregation of additional master data fields during Activity Based Consolidation of Investments (ABCOI) – As of release 2502 a new column “Aggregate on Activity-Based COI” has been introduced to the additional master data field configuration. This provides flexibility in defining the level of granularity of ABCOI.
    More information: Aggregate Additional Master Data Fields During Activity-Based Consolidation of Investments.

  • Support financial statement item and consolidation unit attribute special version assignment to Group Reporting Preparation Ledger (GRPL) – As of release 2502 you can assign a “Consolidation Unit Attribute Version” as optional attribute to a GRPL configuration. Together with the fiscal year and period of an accounting posting, these GRPL attributes are used to identify time- and version-dependent FS item or consolidation unit attribute values which are used in the precondition of a substitution rule in the business context “GRPL core fields” or “GRPL sub-assignments to FS item” for accounting postings.
    More information: Configuration for Group Reporting Preparation Ledger.

  • Support of additional consolidation hierarchy types and other changes in the "Manage Consolidation Hierarchies" app – As of release 2502, the following additional hierarchy types are supported in the “Manage Consolidation Hierarchies” app: Consolidation Billing Document Type, Consolidation Distribution Channel, Consolidation Division, Consolidation Industry, Consolidation Plant, Consolidation Product Sold Group, Consolidation Sales District.
    More information: Manage Consolidation Hierarchies.

  • Import Consolidation Master Data app now supports all master data types, and further improvements –Before release 2502 there were two apps for consolidation master data import. With 2502, there is only a single app: all master data types, i.e. consolidation units, consolidation groups, consolidation group structures, FS items, and all additional master data fields, which are configured as master data enabled in the additional master data field configuration, can now be imported via the “Import Consolidation Master Data” app.
    More information: Import Consolidation Master Data.

  • Partner Fund available as additional industry scenario field for public sector – With release 2502, an additional industry specific field for public sector is available in Group Reporting five industry specific fields for public sector were made available in group reporting: Partner Fund. This field complements the already existing ones dedicated to the public sector: Financial Management Area, Fund, Grant, Cash Origin Chart of Accounts, Cash Origin Account.
    More information: Additional Field for Public Sector

--
For more information

  • Group reporting 2502 feature deck
  • Group reporting 2502 help portal
  • Recap of group reporting new features
  • Group reporting community
  • Group reporting customer influence platform

entry.title: Prepare Like a Pro: Your Guide to SAP Generative AI Certification - C_AIG
entry.content:
The SAP Certified Associate - SAP Generative AI Developer (C_AIG) certification is a recognized certificate that confirms your competence in leveraging SAP's advanced Generative AI technology. This certification provides the skills necessary to create, build, and integrate AI-driven solutions inside SAP ecosystems as AI transforms industries. Preparing for this examination requires a planned approach due to the broad scope and complexity of topics addressed.

Step 1: Understand the Exam Structure and Key Topics

Before diving into your preparation, it’s crucial to familiarize yourself with the exam structure and the weightage of each topic. The C_AIG certification exam focuses on four main areas:

  1. Large Language Models (LLMs) – 21% to 30%
    • Understanding the fundamentals of LLMs.
    • Techniques for applying LLMs to real-world use cases.
    • SAP’s approach to integrating LLMs into business processes.
  2. SAP AI Core – 21% to 30%
    • Learning how to use SAP AI Core on the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP).
    • Managing AI workflows, models, and deployments.
  3. SAP Business AI – 11% to 20%
    • Exploring SAP’s Business AI portfolio.
    • Understanding how AI enhances business processes and decision-making.
  4. SAP Generative AI Hub – 31% to 40%
    • Solving business problems using prompts and LLMs in SAP’s Generative AI Hub.
    • Practical applications of Generative AI in SAP systems.

Pro Tip: Start by reviewing the official SAP Learning Journeys for each topic. These resources are tailored to the exam content and provide a solid foundation for your preparation.

Step 2: Gather the Right Study Resources

To succeed in this exam, you’ll need a mix of theoretical knowledge and hands-on experience. Here are some recommended resources:

  1. Official SAP Learning Journeys:
    • Large Language Models (LLMs)
    • SAP AI Core
    • SAP Business AI
    • SAP Generative AI Hub
  2. Hands-On Practice:
    • Access SAP BTP and experiment with SAP AI Core and Generative AI Hub.
    • Work on real-world use cases to understand how these tools integrate with SAP systems.
  3. Study Mind Maps:
    • I’ve created mind maps to organize and visualize key concepts. These are based on my interpretation of the study material, so I encourage you to use them as a supplement to the official resources. You can find them attached.

Disclaimer: While my mind maps are designed to help, they are not a substitute for SAP’s official learning materials. Always refer to the latest and most relevant content on learning.sap.com.

Step 3: Create and Use Study Mind Maps

Mind maps are an excellent tool for breaking down complex topics into manageable, interconnected concepts. Here’s how you can create and use them effectively:

  1. Start with a Central Topic:
    • For example, “Large Language Models (LLMs).”
  2. Branch Out with Subtopics:
    • Add branches for key areas like “Fundamentals,” “Use Cases,” and “Integration with SAP.”
  3. Add Details and Examples:
    • Under each subtopic, include definitions, examples, and links to relevant SAP documentation.
  4. Use Visual Aids:
    • Incorporate colors, icons, and diagrams to make the mind map visually engaging.

Example Mind Map for LLMs:

  • Central Topic: Large Language Models (LLMs)
    • Branch 1: Fundamentals
      • Definition of LLMs
      • How LLMs work
    • Branch 2: Use Cases
      • Customer service automation
      • Document summarization
    • Branch 3: Integration with SAP
      • SAP Generative AI Hub
      • SAP AI Core workflows

By organizing your study material into mind maps, you’ll find it easier to recall information during the exam.

Step 4: Develop a Study Plan and Manage Your Time

Time management is critical when preparing for the C_AIG exam. Here’s how to create an effective study plan:

  1. Set a Timeline:
    • Determine how many weeks or months you have before the exam and allocate time for each topic.
  2. Prioritize High-Weightage Topics:
    • Focus on SAP Generative AI Hub and LLMs, as they carry the highest weightage.
  3. Schedule Hands-On Practice:
    • Dedicate time each week to experiment with SAP AI Core and Generative AI Hub.
  4. Take Practice Exams:
    • Use practice tests to assess your knowledge and identify areas for improvement.

Step 5: Gain Hands-On Experience

The C_AIG exam tests not only your theoretical knowledge but also your ability to apply it in real-world scenarios. Here’s how to gain hands-on experience:

  1. Access SAP BTP:
    • Set up a free tier account on SAP BTP to explore SAP AI Core and Generative AI Hub.
  2. Work on Use Cases:
    • Try building a chatbot using LLMs or automating a business process with SAP AI Core.
  3. Experiment with Prompts:
    • Practice creating and refining prompts in the Generative AI Hub to solve specific business problems.

Step 6: Stay Motivated and Confident

Preparing for a certification exam can be challenging, but staying motivated is key to success. Here are some tips:

  1. Set Small Goals:
    • Break your study plan into smaller, achievable goals and celebrate each milestone.
  2. Join Study Groups:
    • Collaborate with peers to share insights, ask questions, and stay accountable.
  3. Participate in Forums:
    • Engage with the SAP community on platforms like SAP Community or LinkedIn to learn from others’ experiences.

Step 7: Collaborate and Share Knowledge

The SAP community thrives on collaboration. Here’s how you can contribute:

  1. Share Study Materials:
    • Post your mind maps, notes, or practice questions on forums or social media.
  2. Join Study Groups:
    • Form or join study groups to discuss challenging topics and share resources.
  3. Participate in Webinars and Events:
    • Attend SAP-led webinars or community events to deepen your understanding.

Conclusion: Take the Leap and Contribute to the SAP Community

The SAP Generative AI certification is more than just a credential—it’s an opportunity to position yourself as a leader in the AI-driven future of SAP systems. By following this guide, leveraging study mind maps, and engaging with the SAP community, you’ll be well-prepared to ace the exam.

So, what are you waiting for? Start your preparation today, and don’t forget to share your insights and resources with the community. Together, we can drive innovation and shape the future of SAP Generative AI.

Call-to-Action:

  • Share your study tips and resources in the comments below.
  • Join the conversation on SAP Community using the hashtag #SAPGenerativeAI.
  • Take the first step toward your certification by exploring the official SAP Learning Journeys.

Good luck, and happy studying!

entry.title: How to share data between a plug-in and the application?
entry.content:
We are running multiple apps and a plugin in Launchpad context. The plugin loads data from an odata service that can be used by the apps as well. Data loading takes quite a while...so i want to be able to provide data from the plugin to the apps - some kind of caching controlled by the plugin.

What is the proposed way to achieve this (ui5 version 1.96)?

There is an undocumented 'EventHub' which we are currently using to pass data from the plugin to the app. (EventHub.last)

Since it is undocumented i am not sure whether we should rely on that feature.

EventBus would be possible but the app might register itself after the plugin has loaded data and triggered the "data loaded"-event.

Data contains sensitive information which i do not want to put to storage.local.

entry.title: Re: First steps to work with SAP Cloud ALM Deployment scenario for SAP ABAP systems (7.40 or higher)
entry.content:
Hi,
following instructions Cloud ALM only instructs TMS to do import of TR based on buffer queue; if there is an issue in the import order how can I modify this?
In the documentation they do not recommend to perform buffer manipulation or manual import.
Usually in a S4 Hana project there many teams that work together on different features and create many TR; QAS or Pre-Prod is used to revise the correct import order for PRD system.

TMS parameters are configured with NO_IMPORT_ALL and IMPORT_SINGLE_ONLY set to value 1,  so it seems import sequence will follow order in transport buffer ("full track" with import of all TR is deactivated).

I know that transport checks can be useful to anticipate conflicts, but if the import in the QAS is only linked to release date (it cannot be changed by Clould ALM and is not recommended to ajust buffer manually), which is the right way to solve and prevent import issues with CALM?

 

Regards 

  Maurizio

entry.title: I like, I wish
entry.content:
Dear course participants,

Your input helps us tremendously in shaping and improving our courses and the platform. This is why we ask all participants to share their thoughts after completing a course.

The “I like, I wish” method allows you to structure your feedback. If you’ve never used it before, see the following examples:

* “I like that the course content was broken down into smaller lessons”.
* “I wish I could log on to learning.sap.com with my LinkedIn credentials”.

Please try and use this approach to share your feedback with us. Although we can’t reply to these posts, we promise to read each and every suggestion and discuss them within the team.

Kind regards,
Britta Radicke-Schlinsog
(SAP Learning team)

entry.title: Re: CCM - Statistical price condition in delta accruals
entry.content:
To resolve this issue, consider the following approaches:
1. Ensure RED1 Carries the Accrual Flag: Check if the pricing procedure correctly passes the accrual flag from REA1 to RED1.
2. Modify Account Key Assignment: Verify the pricing schema to ensure that the accrual account key is used instead of the G/L account key.
3. Custom Condition Type: If standard settings don’t work, define a custom condition type that explicitly enables accrual postings.
4. Adjust Settlement Document Configuration: Confirm that 0S42 correctly maps statistical conditions for accrual postings.
5. Check Account Determination Rules: Review settings in OBYC and VKOA to ensure correct mapping of accrual-related accounts.
6. Debug Account Determination: Use SLG1/ST22 logs to analyze missing postings and identify root causes.
7. Use a BAdI or User Exit: Implement BADI_SD_REV_ACC_DET to enforce accrual postings dynamically

entry.title: Re: Different timeout for the same report in Fiori
entry.content:
Hi!

The issue is resolved. On the frontend server, I changed the parameter - icm/server_port_2 PROT=HTTPS,PORT=443,TIMEOUT=60,PROCTIMEOUT=10800

entry.title: The depreciation under the Written Down Value (WDV) method was incorrectly calculated as per the pre
entry.content: The depreciation under the Written Down Value (WDV) method was incorrectly calculated as per the prescribed percentage under the Indian Companies Act.The customer applied the same depreciation percentage for the WDV method across all years, whereas SAP calculated depreciation based on the remaining useful life, leading to a difference. Please provide a solution to address this discrepancy.

entry.title: Re: What are the limitations to the number of columns a v...
entry.content:
Hej, there is a limit of 64000 columns for a column store table.
I'd like to point out 3 things you might want to consider:
1.) if you run GraphScript algorithms on your graph, it may be ok to include just a minimum number of columns (key columns only), and then add additional data to the result of the algorithm using a relational join afterwards.
2.) you might want to take a look at the HANA JSON document store as a backend for your heterogenous graph. Note that there is currently no OpenCypher support for "Graph on DocStore". 
3.) for the upcoming release, we plan to support an RDF triplestore and SPARQL. This might be a good alternative to run pattern matching queries on heterogenous graphs.

entry.title: Re: Restricting Enterprise Search - S_ESH_CONN

entry.title: Re: Restricting Enterprise Search - S_ESH_CONN
entry.content: Hi Kevin, you need to determine what type of ESH connectors you have. You can see them in table ESH_ADM_STATUS or in transaction ESH_COCKPIT (here you can also see the description of each connector, as it will show in Fiori). Any connector starting with CD$ is a CDS connector governed by SDDLVIEW. Any connector starting with <SID><client> is a classic connector governed by S_ESH_CONN. Knowing that, please check what type of connectors you have in each system, and what authorizations, to make sense of it. to reiterate: S_ESH_CONN only controls classic connectors and SDDLVIEW only controls CDS connectors.

entry.title: Re: ASAPIO Addon - Event Messaging - ECC - Payload fields
entry.content:
Hi,
Inside the ASAPIO Integration Add-On, you can use DATA EVENTS to configure the Add-On to also send data from the object. To specify which data the ASAPIO Integration Add-On should send, you can use a database table, database view, CDS view, or the Payload Designer.

The following documentation explains how to configure a DATA EVENT:
Configuring a DATA EVENT

Kind Regards,
Benedikt

entry.title: Re: Restricting Enterprise Search - S_ESH_CONN
entry.content: to answer your other question, if you deactivate a connector in ESH_COCKPIT, that connector is not available for searching anymore, and it won't appear in the Fiori search drop down. A search on All on Fiori will not search on that connector. The system will behave as if you had deleted the connector, but it is not deleted, just deactivated.

            logger.info("query completed!");

entry.title: Re: CALM for implementation - Import order of many TR tha...
entry.content:
Hey Maurizio,

I think it would help the readers if you would give us a concrete example.

In your question you mention you have a feature... so it's implied you only have one feature. If this is true, you should never have a sequence issue that requires manual intervention (if you do, please explain why, as I cannot imagine how).

Now, what I could imagine to be a problem is if we have 2 features, feat A and feat B.

  1. You change object XYZ with feat A, TR 1, you push to test and release
  2. You change object XYZ with feat B, TR 2, you push to test and release
  3. You change object XYZ with feat A again, TR 3, you push to test and release

Now we have a problem. If you push feat A first, you cannot push feat B anymore as you will have regression issues. And in fact, pushing feat A will result in predecessor issues, as TR 2 will be missing.

I'm not sure what happens if we tell the system to push both features simultaneously... if CALM will take all the TR's from both features, we are good, as the system will trigger the import of all 3 TR's at the same time (I will test this today, now I'm curious). If the system will do something else as submitting 2 separate requests to import, one for each feat, this is an issue for sure.

In any case, it's hopefully an issue that will be resolved this quarter with the scheduled transports (which hopefully is similar to what we have in SolMan with the change cycles.

Cheers!
Bruno

entry.title: Re: what is reconciliation accounts?
entry.content: The reconciliation account in SAP S/4HANA is an important concept used to manage and monitor financial transactions between a main account and its associated subledger accounts. In theoretic accounting, it is also commonly referred to as a control account.
